2605.26099.title: Language Models Need Sleep
2605.26099.paper_summary: Transformer-based large language models are increasingly used for long-horizon tasks; however, their attention mechanism scales poorly with context length. To handle this, we study a sleep-like consolidation mechanism in which a model periodically converts recent context into persistent fast weights before clearing its key-value cache. During sleep, the model performs $N$ offline recurrent passes over the accumulated context and updates the fast weights in its state-space model (SSM) blocks through a learned local rule. During inference, this shifts extra computation to sleep while preserving the latency of wake-time prediction. We test our method on controlled synthetic tasks, including cellular automata and multi-hop graph retrieval, as well as a realistic math reasoning task, on which a regular transformer as well as SSM-attention hybrid models fail. We then show that increasing sleep duration $N$ for our models improves performance, with the largest gains on examples that require deeper reasoning.
2605.26099.paper_summary_zh: 基於Transformer的大型語言模型越來越多地用於長期任務；然而，它們的注意力機制在上下文長度上擴展不佳。為了解決這個問題，我們研究了一種類似於睡眠的整合機制，在該機制中，模型定期將最近的上下文轉換為持久的快速權重，然後清除其鍵值緩存。在睡眠期間，模型對累積的上下文進行 $N$ 次離線重複遍歷，並通過學習的局部規則更新其狀態空間模型（SSM）區塊中的快速權重。在推理期間，這將額外的計算轉移到睡眠中，同時保持清醒時間預測的延遲。我們在受控的合成任務上測試我們的方法，包括細胞自動機和多跳圖檢索，以及一個現實的數學推理任務，常規Transformer和SSM-注意力混合模型在這些任務上失敗。我們接著展示，增加我們模型的睡眠時間 $N$ 可以提高性能，尤其是在需要更深層推理的例子上獲得最大的增益。
2605.26099.author: Sangyun Lee et.al.
2605.26099.authors: Sangyun Lee, Sean McLeish, Tom Goldstein, Giulia Fanti
